name: agent-86
description: Local LLM agentic coding assistant with file read/write and terminal approval

commands.title: Re-detect Tool Support for Active Model

agent86.baseUrl.description: Base URL for the OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint

agent86.maxContextTokens.description: Maximum context tokens for the model

agent86.braveApiKey.description: Brave Search API key for the web_search tool. Can also be set via the BRAVE_API_KEY environment variable.

agent86.maxToolRounds.description: Maximum number of tool-calling rounds per inference cycle before the model is asked to produce a final answer.

agent86.customSystemPrompt.description: Custom text appended to the system prompt on every request. Use it for standing instructions (style, conventions, constraints).

agent86.maxToolRoundsPerPlanItem.description: Maximum tool-calling rounds per plan step before the step is asked to wrap up in plain text.

agent86.verifyPlanItems.description: After each plan step, run a one-shot verification call that judges whether the step was completed. A failed verdict retries the step once with the critique injected.

agent86.toolResultEvictionThresholdChars.description: When the conversation history exceeds this many characters, tool results outside the most recent rounds are stubbed down to one line to keep context small. Set 0 to disable.

agent86.toolResultEvictionKeepRounds.description: Number of most recent tool rounds whose results are never evicted.

agent86.modelTier.description: Model tier for tool selection policy. 'small' enforces strict search-first with aggressive recovery (9B-class models). 'balanced' is the default for 35B-class models. 'high' allows broader exploration for capable hosted models.

agent86.preserveReasoning.description: Echo the model's prior reasoning_content back on assistant messages in multi-turn tool loops. Leave OFF for DeepSeek and most models: DeepSeek's docs advise against re-sending reasoning, and doing so changes the cached request prefix and collapses prompt-cache hit rates. Enable ONLY for models that require preserved reasoning (e.g. Kimi-for-Coding).

agent86.shell.description: Shell used for the execute_bash tool. 'auto' prefers PowerShell 7+ (pwsh), then Windows PowerShell (powershell.exe), then cmd.exe on Windows / the system shell elsewhere. Pin a specific shell to override. Affects both how commands run and the command guidance given to the model.

agent86.providers.markdownDescription: Legacy combined provider+model list. Superseded by `#agent86.providerConnections#` and `#agent86.models#`; kept only for one-time migration.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the OpenAI-compatible endpoint

agent86.providerConnections.description: Provider connections (endpoint + credentials). Each can host many models.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the endpoint, e.g. 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'

agent86.models.description: Configured models, each attached to a provider connection.

connectionId.description: Id of the provider connection this model runs on

model.description: Model id sent to the API, e.g. 'deepseek/deepseek-chat'

label.description: Optional display name shown in the model picker

openRouterProvider.description: Optional OpenRouter upstream provider to pin (no fallback)

scripts.package: npm install && npm run typecheck && vsce package
